name: WeChat MCP Server
what_it_does: WeChat MCP server enabling AI agents to interact with WeChat messaging — sending messages to contacts and groups, reading conversations, managing WeChat contacts, and integrating China's dominant personal messaging platform into agent-driven personal assistant and communication workflows.
use_cases: Sending personal messages and notifications via WeChat from personal assistant agents
Reading and summarizing WeChat conversations from communication agents
Monitoring WeChat group messages for important updates from alerting agents
Automating WeChat-based customer service from business agents
Forwarding AI-generated content to WeChat contacts from content agents
Managing WeChat communications from productivity agents
not_for: Teams not in China or without Chinese contacts on WeChat
Mass messaging (violates WeChat ToS and risks account ban)
best_when: An agent needs to send personal WeChat messages in China-focused workflows — for personal assistants, notifications to Chinese contacts, or WeChat-integrated automation.
avoid_when: You need enterprise messaging (use WeCom MCP), international messaging (use Slack/Discord), or must avoid WeChat ToS risks from automation.

auth.notes: Uses WeChat desktop/web client automation or itchat library. Requires WeChat account logged in. QR code scan authentication to WeChat account. No formal API auth.

pricing.notes: Free to use. Requires personal WeChat account. Uses itchat or similar library that accesses WeChat web client. No official WeChat API used for personal accounts.

performance.rate_limits: WeChat informal rate limits; automated access may trigger account restrictions

known_agent_gotchas: Uses itchat/unofficial WeChat API — WeChat actively blocks automation; high account ban risk
WeChat account requires QR code re-scan periodically — automated workflows may break
WeChat ToS prohibits automated messaging — account termination risk
Community MCP using unofficial API — WeChat changes break this regularly
Data goes through Tencent servers in China — data sovereignty implications
Only works with personal WeChat accounts that can access WeChat Web (some accounts restricted)
agent_readiness.security_notes: Unofficial WeChat API — high ToS violation risk. Account ban risk for automation. Data in China (Tencent). Community MCP. Personal account access — high-risk for production use. Consider WeCom for legitimate business messaging.

security_score_components.security_notes: Unofficial API — ToS risk. Account ban risk. Data in China. Community MCP. Consider WeCom for business use.
